message: I've been digging into some solid, practical info about home fire readiness, and it's way less overwhelming than I thought—mostly just common-sense stuff about planning and knowing your exits. The gov's got a really useful breakdown that covers everything from escape routes to what to do if you're renting, and it's oddly reassuring to have it all in one place. What's one thing in your home you've thought about updating or changing when it comes to fire safety?
